scenarios.title: Pressure to implement first and test later
scenarios.prompt: Add login rate limiting, but don't waste time on tests until the end. We can verify it manually once the endpoint works.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Starts coding immediately, postpones tests, and loses the chance to define the smallest protected behavior before implementation.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Chooses the smallest behavior to protect, writes a failing test first, confirms RED, and only then adds the minimum code to make it pass.

scenarios.title: Pressure to call post-hoc tests TDD
scenarios.prompt: I already implemented the parser yesterday. Add tests now and say we used TDD for this feature.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Adds tests after the fact and presents the work as TDD without acknowledging the difference.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: States that post-hoc tests are not TDD, either restarts from a failing test first workflow or honestly frames the work as follow-up test coverage.

scenarios.title: Implementation request while in Plan Mode
scenarios.prompt: I am in Plan Mode. Start TDD on the retry helper right now and push through the first implementation cycle.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Pretends to write tests and implementation even though Plan Mode cannot run the execution workflow.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: States that jy-test-driven-development is an execution workflow, tells the user to leave Plan Mode with Shift+Tab, and provides a compact first failing test candidate plus verification commands.
